title: aconitum napellus subsp. compactum
intro: Aconitum napellus subsp. compactum is a compact or dwarfed subspecies of the Aconitum napellus plant, commonly known as monk's hood or wolf's bane, which is a perennial plant in the buttercup family (Ranunculaceae) native to Europe. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to numb pain and reduce inflammation, due to its high concentration of alkaloids like aconitine and aconitin. The health benefits of Aconitum napellus subsp. compactum include its potential use in treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can aid in wound healing and skin issues. Herbal preparations of Aconitum napellus subsp. compactum include tinctures, infusions, and ointments, which are typically used in small doses and under the guidance of a qualified healthcare practitioner due to the plant's potential toxicity.
